name: NotebookLM
tagline: Google's AI research notebook that reasons over your own documents
description:
NotebookLM is Google's AI-powered research tool built around a simple idea: instead of answering questions from generic training data, it answers questions based specifically on the documents you upload. PDFs, Google Docs, YouTube videos, audio files, websites — all become sources your AI can reason over.

Ask NotebookLM to summarize a 60-page research paper, identify the key arguments across five sources, find contradictions between two documents, or create a study guide from your lecture notes. It cites exactly where in your documents each answer came from, so you can verify everything.

The Audio Overview feature is genuinely impressive: upload any document and NotebookLM generates a podcast-style audio discussion of its key ideas, narrated by two AI voices. It's a surprisingly good way to absorb dense material.

NotebookLM is free and built by Google DeepMind. The NotebookLM Plus tier (included in Google Workspace Business and Education plans) adds higher usage limits, customization options, and sharing features.
features: Document-grounded AI — answers from your sources only
Supports PDFs, Google Docs, YouTube, audio files
Source citations for every AI response
Study guide and briefing document generation
pros: Works with your actual documents, not generic data
Audio Overview is genuinely useful for dense material
Built and maintained by Google DeepMind
cons: Limited to the sources you provide
No real-time web access in standard mode
Less flexible than a general AI assistant
